fact: December 21 is the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere - the shortest day of the year! Some places get less than 6 hours of sunlight.
In Antarctica, December 21 brings 24 hours of blazing sunlight because it's their summer solstice - no bedtime needed!
Reindeer in the Arctic have special eyes that change color from gold in summer to blue in winter, and by December 21 they're at their bluest!
December 21 marks when bears in cold regions are deep in hibernation, and their heart rate drops to just 8 beats per minute - yours is probably 100!
On this date, evergreen trees like pine and spruce are pumping extra antifreeze chemicals through their needles to survive freezing temperatures.
The sun sets so early on December 21 in northern places that kids in Alaska might see sunset during lunch time!
December 21 falls right at the border between Sagittarius and Capricorn zodiac signs - people born today are cosmic border-crossers!
Penguins in Antarctica are having their wildest party season around December 21 because it's their warmest, brightest time of year for mating.
Snow crystals forming around December 21 in freezing climates create the most perfect six-sided shapes because the air is super cold and still.
December 21 is exactly 10 days before New Year's Eve - you could count down on your fingers and toes!
Owls in snowy regions hunt almost all day long around December 21 because their prey is desperately searching for food in the harsh winter.
The Earth is actually closest to the sun in late December, even though it's winter in the north - we're about 3 million miles closer than in summer!
